It still auto-creates missing intake mailboxes + renews the configured ones. A mailbox REMOVAL no longer needs manual deletion (was: the digital@ manual-delete gap).

mailboxRbac.sourceCommand: info@/desk@ effective access proven by a SUCCESSFUL POST /subscriptions (create-missing returned created:[info@,desk@], errors:[]) at 2026-06-29T15:38Z, after the Exchange-RBAC permission cache cleared. (Direct GET /users/<mbx>/messages deliberately not re-run, to avoid extra Graph calls during the cache-sensitive window.)
mailboxRbac.docDrift: 2026-06-29T15:39Z: info@ + desk@ are now Exchange-RBAC EFFECTIVE (scope CollisionSpike-Intake-Prod, Application Mail.Read) — their Graph push-subscription creates SUCCEEDED with zero ErrorAccessDenied, flipping them from the prior 403. The earlier 403s were the ~30min-2h permission cache, not a wrong grant (Test-ServicePrincipalAuthorization already showed InScope:True). digital@ remains scoped but is dropped from the intake config. Production mailbox set info@ + engineers@ + desk@ is now fully scoped + subscribed.

value.rootCause: Flex Consumption does not wake a scaled-to-zero app for a plain timer trigger, so graph-renew (NCRONTAB) logged 0 executions in 7d and subscriptions were heading for a silent lapse (azure-diagnostician root-caused, App Insights component cespk-orch-dev).
value.fix: Durable eternal-orchestration subscriptionMonitorOrchestrator (instanceId subscription-monitor-singleton, runtimeStatus Running): renew activity -> durable timer (SUBSCRIPTION_MONITOR_INTERVAL_HOURS, default 6h) -> continueAsNew. A durable timer message WAKES a scaled-to-zero Flex app (Microsoft Learn), so renewal fires unattended with NO always-ready instance and NO external scheduler. Bootstrapped by intake-starter (any intake traffic) and by the graph-renew HTTP route; the original graph-renew timer is retained as a backstop.

subscriptionRenewalRisk.sourceCommand: durable mgmt GET /runtime/webhooks/durabletask/instances/subscription-monitor-singleton (runtimeStatus Running, name subscriptionMonitorOrchestrator); also confirmed via POST /api/graph-renew JSON monitor.status=Running
subscriptionRenewalRisk.note: OPERATOR WATCH (next cycle): confirm an UNATTENDED renew fires at the durable-timer wake (~6h cadence) i.e. graph-renewal-success in App Insights with no manual trigger. 2026-06-29T15:39Z: the singleton was re-confirmed Running during the mailbox cutover (graph-renew HTTP returned monitor.status=Running) and it now covers all 3 production subs (it renews EVERY existing sub). DURABILITY GAP (recommended small code fix): runSubscriptionMaintenance create-misses + renews but never PRUNES — a mailbox REMOVED from GRAPH_INTAKE_MAILBOXES keeps its subscription (renewed forever), which is exactly why the de-scoped digital@ sub had to be deleted by hand here. Recommend adding a prune step to runSubscriptionMaintenance (subscriptions.ts): after create+renew, delete any of OUR subs whose mailboxOfResource(resource) is not in intakeMailboxes(), so a GRAPH_INTAKE_MAILBOXES change fully self-reconciles (add+remove) on the next maintenance tick. Belt-and-braces renewal fallback if the durable timer ever fails: az functionapp scale config always-ready set -g rg-collisionspike-dev -n cespk-orch-dev --settings function:graph-renew=1 (always-ready cost — flagged, NOT enabled).

deployments.dataProcessing: GlobalStandard = inference may process in ANY Azure region (data at rest stays uksouth); no UK data zone exists (DataZone is US/EU only) and uksouth has no regional-Standard gpt-5 quota — Global is the only gpt-5 option here
